subject: Databases
title: The CAP Theorem and NoSQL
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The CAP Theorem and NoSQL.

hook.text: Pick two: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance. The CAP theorem defines the trade-offs for distributed NoSQL.

buildup.text: Partition tolerance is non-negotiable in distributed systems. Networks always fail eventually.

discovery.text: The real choice: consistency (reject requests if data might be stale) or availability (always respond, maybe stale).

twist.text: Cassandra chooses availability. MongoDB chooses consistency. DynamoDB lets you choose per query.

climax.text: In practice, CAP is a spectrum, not a binary choice. Tunable consistency blurs the lines.

punchline.text: CAP forces a trade-off. Know which one your database makes.

quiz.question: In the CAP theorem, why is partition tolerance non-negotiable?
options: Network failures are inevitable in distributed systems
The theorem requires all three properties to be met
Partition tolerance is the easiest property to achieve
